## SECTION 1 — Global Pulse (Geopolitics & World Economy)
The global geopolitical architecture has experienced a severe destabilization over the last 24 hours as the fragile ceasefire in the Middle East collapsed entirely. Following a deadly Hezbollah drone strike that penetrated northern Israeli defenses, the Israel Defense Forces initiated massive, kinetic airstrikes across southern Lebanon. Concurrently, the Strait of Hormuz remains functionally paralyzed following direct weekend naval skirmishes between United States Central Command and Iranian maritime assets. This absolute disruption of the world's most critical energy chokepoint has forced Brent crude to sustain aggressively above $106 per barrel. Diplomatic backchannels in Geneva report that Tehran is demanding immediate, sweeping sanctions relief to de-escalate, terms that Washington has firmly rejected, leaving global markets bracing for a prolonged period of severe energy-driven stagflation.

## SECTION 2 — Global News (Complete Continental Coverage)
### Americas
In a highly unprecedented and massive security breach in Washington D.C., the United States Secret Service was forced to evacuate President Donald Trump from the Washington Hilton on Saturday night. An armed suspect carrying firearms and edged weapons stormed the hotel lobby just outside the White House Correspondents' Dinner before being aggressively neutralized by federal agents. The incident has completely dominated the domestic political cycle, prompting emergency reviews of presidential security perimeters.
### Europe
The European Union has officially escalated its Emergency Energy Rationing Protocol to Phase 2 in response to the indefinite blockage of the Strait of Hormuz. Heavy industrial manufacturing hubs across Germany, Italy, and France are now legally mandated to enforce a 20% reduction in power consumption to preserve remaining liquified natural gas (LNG) reserves. This mandate has sparked widespread labor protests across major European capitals as factory shutdowns threaten immediate, massive layoffs.
### Asia-Pacific
The Governments of India and New Zealand officially signed a historic, comprehensive Free Trade Agreement (FTA) today in New Delhi, fundamentally reshaping Indo-Pacific commerce. The sweeping bilateral pact grants 100% duty-free access for Indian exports to New Zealand and immediately slashes tariffs on 95% of incoming New Zealand goods. Economic analysts project this will trigger a massive expansion in Indian pharmaceutical and IT service exports to the Oceanic region.
### Africa
The strategic maritime corridor surrounding the Cape of Good Hope is currently buckling under unprecedented, catastrophic congestion. Hundreds of commercial supertankers and container ships, forcibly diverted from the Red Sea, are anchored off the South African coastline awaiting critical refueling and armed security escorts. This massive diversion has completely overwhelmed local maritime infrastructure, triggering severe, localized fuel shortages and port paralysis in Cape Town.

In a historic and highly classified demonstration of autonomous orbital defense, SpaceX’s recently activated xAI logistics node successfully repelled a coordinated, state-sponsored cyber-intrusion targeting the Starlink constellation. The Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology in India officially proposed the stringent "Online Gaming Rules 2026" today to heavily regulate the explosive digital betting sector. The Archaeological Survey of India (ASI) has officially commenced a highly delicate, historic engineering intervention at the 13th-century Konark Sun Temple in Odisha. An absolute political earthquake struck the Rajya Sabha today as seven prominent Aam Aadmi Party (AAP) MPs, including Raghav Chadha and Harbhajan Singh, officially defected to the ruling Bharatiya Janata Party (BJP). This massive political realignment pushes the BJP's Upper House tally to 113 MPs, significantly altering the legislative balance of power. AAP leadership immediately submitted a fierce petition seeking their absolute disqualification under the anti-defection law. Simultaneously, Prime Minister Narendra Modi heavily escalated his election campaign in West Bengal, promising to eradicate regional syndicate corruption.

**Possible Long-Term Consequences:** Forced, painful deglobalization of the energy sector, imminent global stagflation, and the aggressive weaponization of domestic green energy transitions for national survival.
